title: Exploring Magnets — Pole Matching Activity
instructions_for_student: Step 1: Fill each blank cell with the correct pole letter (N or S) using the dropdown. Step 2: Drag a line from each row in Column I to either Attraction or Repulsion in Column II. Use the rule: unlike poles attract, like poles repel.

matchingData.title: Poles — Match & Fill
matchingData.subtitle: Step 1: Fill the blanks (N or S). Step 2: Draw lines from Column I to Column II.

schema.description: Configuration schema for MatchingTool — a two-phase interactive activity. Phase 1: fill blank pole letters from a dropdown. Phase 2: draw lines from pole pairs to Attraction/Repulsion. Themed with Poppins and the Singularity design system (#533086, #FC9145, #4A4DC9).

title.description: Heading shown at top of tool.

rows.description: Each row is one Column I entry with two pole chips and an outcome.

left.description: Left pole value ('N', 'S', or '' if blank).

right.description: Right pole value ('N', 'S', or '' if blank).

leftBlank.description: true = left pole is a fill-in blank.

rightBlank.description: true = right pole is a fill-in blank.

correctLeft.description: Correct answer for left pole (used for validation).

correctRight.description: Correct answer for right pole (used for validation).

outcome.description: The correct Column II match for this row.

agent_readme.overview: MatchingTool is a two-phase drag-and-match activity. Phase 1 asks students to fill blank pole cells via a dropdown (N or S). Wrong fills bounce back immediately with a hint. Phase 2 asks students to drag lines from each completed row to the correct outcome column. Correct lines lock green; incorrect lines bounce back with the unlike-poles rule as a hint. A completion modal shows the full matched table.

design_system.no_external_deps: Uses only React + lucide-react. No react-dom, no portals.

mechanics.fill_phase: Students click a blank chip to open a mini dropdown (N/S). Wrong answer triggers shake animation and clears after 700ms. Correct answer locks the chip green.
mechanics.match_phase: Once all blanks in a row are correctly filled, a pulsing dot appears on the right edge. Students drag from this dot to an outcome. Correct match: green SVG cubic-bezier line locks. Wrong match: dashed red line bounces back after 800ms.
mechanics.hint: Both wrong fills and wrong matches show a floating orange hint bubble for 3 seconds: 'Unlike poles attract, like poles repel.'
mechanics.completion: When all rows are correctly matched, a modal shows the full N/S table with Attraction/Repulsion labels and a Try Again button.

tool_call_examples.description: All four rows pre-filled, no blanks — pure matching exercise

matchingData.title: Poles — Match Only
matchingData.subtitle: Draw a line from each pole pair to Attraction or Repulsion.

tool_call_examples.description: All four blanks — fill-only challenge, then match

matchingData.title: Fill & Match — Full Challenge
matchingData.subtitle: Fill all four blanks first, then draw the lines.

decision_tree.standard_exercise: Use default parameters — 4 rows, 2 blanks (r2 right, r4 left).
decision_tree.no_blanks: Set all leftBlank and rightBlank to false — pure drag-to-match.
decision_tree.all_blanks: Set all leftBlank and rightBlank to true — maximum challenge.
decision_tree.different_topic: Replace rows with any left/right label pairs (not just pole letters) and update outcomes.
